senses.definition: the ways through which people receive information such as newspapers, television, etc.

examples.x: The media plays a crucial role in shaping public opinion and disseminating information on current events.
The event provided comprehensive insight into the unfolding developments and their broader implications through media coverage.
Media literacy education is essential in helping people critically evaluate information and navigate the vast landscape of media sources available today.
The advertising industry heavily relies on the media to reach target audiences and promote products and services.
She studies how the media influences politics and public opinion.

senses.definition: a section or part in a newspaper in which people are given advice regarding their personal problems

examples.x: I always enjoy reading the advice column in my favorite magazine for tips on managing stress.
The advice column often tackles difficult topics like finances and relationships.
After a tough breakup, I turned to the advice column for some guidance on how to move on.
I always look forward to the advice column because it offers practical solutions to everyday problems.
I found some helpful suggestions in the advice column about dealing with family conflicts.

senses.definition: a short pause in a TV or radio program for advertisement

examples.x: During the break in the program, viewers are typically shown a series of advertisements for products and services.
Some advertisers strategically time their commercials to coincide with breaks in popular TV shows to reach a larger audience.

senses.definition: to use airwaves to send out TV or radio programs

examples.x: The television network broadcasts news programs every evening.
The radio station broadcasts music and talk shows throughout the day.
The cable channel broadcasts documentaries on nature and wildlife.
Can you broadcast the sports events live to subscribers?
The internet radio station is broadcasting music from various genres 24/7.

senses.definition: a TV or radio program

examples.x: Before the internet era, families would gather around the radio to listen to their favorite broadcasts of dramas and comedy shows.
Television networks often schedule live broadcasts of major sporting events, attracting large audiences of sports fans.
The radio station announced that it would be hosting a special broadcast to commemorate the anniversary of the historic event.

senses.definition: a TV station that broadcasts different programs

examples.x: The local channel airs a mix of community events, weather updates, and locally produced programs.
Television networks compete for viewership by offering exclusive programs and innovative channel packages.

senses.definition: an advertisement broadcast on TV or radio

examples.x: The company's new product was promoted through a series of television commercials.
She starred in a commercial for a popular brand of shampoo.
The radio commercial for the upcoming concert attracted a large audience.

senses.definition: the specific form or version that a book, magazine, or similar publication is in
to produce a newspaper, book, etc. for the public to purchase

examples.x: The company published a new novel last month.
They publish a daily newspaper with the latest news.
The author finally published his first book.
She hopes to publish her research findings soon.
The university press publishes academic journals regularly.

senses.definition: to make a book, newspaper, or magazine ready for publication through revision, correction, etc.

examples.x: The editor will carefully edit the manuscript to enhance its structure and readability.
Journalists often edit their articles to ensure accuracy and adherence to editorial guidelines.
The author collaborated with the editor to edit and refine the book before publication.
As part of the publishing process, professionals edit the text to eliminate errors and inconsistencies.
The magazine editor edited the story to make it more concise.

senses.definition: one part of a series of a radio or TV show

examples.x: The latest episode of the podcast delved into the history of ancient civilizations, captivating listeners with its engaging storytelling.
Fans eagerly awaited the premiere of the new episode of their favorite TV drama, eager to see how the plot would unfold.
Each episode of the documentary series explores a different aspect of wildlife conservation, shedding light on the efforts to protect endangered species.
The radio show's host introduced the guest for the evening's episode, promising an insightful discussion on current events and politics.

senses.definition: the first and main page of a newspaper in which important news pieces are printed

examples.x: The shocking news made it to the front page of the newspaper.
The front page featured a photo of the president’s visit to the city.
His story was so important that it was printed on the front page.
The front page of the magazine showcased the latest fashion trends.
She was excited to see her article on the front page.

senses.definition: to provide a report on or talk about an event in a news piece or media

examples.x: The journalist covered the presidential inauguration, providing live updates throughout the day.
The news channel covered the local election extensively, interviewing candidates and analyzing voter turnout.
The newspaper covered the groundbreaking ceremony for the new hospital, highlighting key speeches.
The reporter covered the charity gala, interviewing attendees and showcasing the evening's highlights.
The media outlet covered the protest rally, capturing the crowd's chants and speeches from various perspectives.

senses.definition: the large words in the upper part of a page of a newspaper, article, etc.

examples.x: The headline of today’s newspaper reads, "Major Earthquake Strikes the City."
The headline caught everyone's attention with its bold statement about the politician’s sudden resignation.
The headline on the front page was so shocking that it immediately became the topic of conversation in the office.
The headline highlighted the economic crisis, urging readers to turn to the inside pages for detailed coverage.
As soon as the headline was published, social media exploded with reactions from readers around the world.

senses.definition: the person in front of a camera who talks about different topics or invites guests to a TV or radio show

examples.x: The host skillfully navigated through diverse topics on the evening talk show.
She earned acclaim as the charismatic host of a popular morning news program.
The host introduced the celebrity guest and asked insightful questions.
As a radio show host, he brought humor and wit to every broadcast.
The host's engaging personality kept the audience tuned in for the entire hour.

senses.definition: to ask someone questions about a particular topic on the TV, radio, or for a newspaper

examples.x: The journalist was eager to interview the renowned scientist for a feature article in the newspaper.
The television host prepared thoughtful questions to interview the author about their latest best-selling book.
The radio reporter traveled to the event to interview the keynote speaker for a live broadcast.
A documentary filmmaker decided to interview local residents to capture their perspectives on the community's history.
They asked insightful questions when they interviewed the artist for the magazine.

senses.definition: a formal meeting during which a journalist asks a famous person different questions about specific subjects for publication

examples.x: The magazine conducted an interview with the actor about his upcoming film.
She gave an exclusive interview to the news outlet to discuss her new book.
The journalist prepared a list of questions for the interview with the CEO of the company.
The interview with the musician revealed insights into her creative process.
The journalist conducted an interview with the politician regarding recent policy changes.

senses.definition: to give details to an audience about the show, guests, etc. that are about to be seen or heard

examples.x: The host introduced the speakers, giving the audience a brief overview of their backgrounds.
The presenter introduced the film, sharing interesting facts about its production and cast.
The announcer introduced the guests, setting the stage for an exciting conversation.
She introduced the show, giving the audience a sneak peek of the upcoming segments.
Before the play started, the director introduced the production team.

senses.definition: a distinct news piece on TV, in a newspaper, etc.

examples.x: The anchor announced the top items of the day during the evening news broadcast.
The newspaper dedicated several items to covering local events and community happenings.
Online news websites often feature trending items to highlight the most talked-about stories of the moment.
The magazine included a special investigative item that exposed corruption within the government.
Viewers tuned in eagerly to catch the latest items on the morning news program.

senses.definition: a magazine or newspaper that gives information about a specific topic

examples.x: He subscribed to a travel journal to get tips for his upcoming vacation.
Many students find academic journals useful for their research projects.
The business journal reported on the recent changes in market trends.
She found a fascinating article in a health journal about new fitness trends.

senses.definition: someone who listens to a radio program, often in a regular manner

examples.x: As a devoted listener, Sarah tunes in to her favorite radio show every morning during her commute to work.
The podcast host values feedback from listeners and often incorporates their suggestions into future episodes.
Radio stations strive to engage their listeners by playing a diverse range of music and hosting interactive segments.
The talk show's host has a loyal following of listeners who appreciate his insightful commentary on current events.
Streaming platforms track listener data to provide personalized recommendations based on individual preferences and listening habits.

senses.definition: (of TV or radio broadcasts) aired at the exact moment the events are taking place, without any earlier recording or editing

examples.x: We watched the live broadcast of the soccer match on TV.
The live feed from the International Space Station showed a breathtaking view of Earth.
The weather reporter gave a live update as the storm approached the coast.
They aired a live interview with the famous actor on the morning show.
The reporters gave live updates from the scene of the accident.
The news channel provided live coverage of the presidential debate.

senses.definition: a group of TV or radio stations broadcasting the same program at the same time in different places

examples.x: The popular show was aired on a national network, reaching millions of viewers.
The network ensures that news is broadcast across multiple regions at the same time.
She landed a job as a correspondent for a major network.
The radio network played the concert live for listeners all over the country.
The sports event was covered by the network, allowing fans everywhere to watch it.

senses.definition: a company or building where TV or radio programs are being broadcast
an article in a TV or radio broadcast or in a magazine or newspaper
a digital audio program that is available for download or streaming on the Internet, typically produced in a series format covering a wide range of topics

examples.x: She listens to a history podcast on her way to work.
They launched a new podcast about technology trends.
His podcast features interviews with famous authors.
He subscribed to a weekly science podcast.
The podcast covers politics, culture, and social issues.

senses.definition: newspapers, journalists, and magazines as a whole

examples.x: He gave an interview to the press about his new book.
The press covered the event extensively, with multiple stories published.
The press often influences public opinion with its coverage of political issues.
After the announcement, the press was quick to report on the reactions.
Public figures are frequently in the spotlight of the press.

senses.definition: someone who reads a certain magazine or newspaper
to cover or give the details of an event in written or spoken form as a journalist on TV, etc.

examples.x: Journalists regularly report on breaking news stories to keep the public informed.
The weather forecast often reports on upcoming changes in atmospheric conditions.
Yesterday, the news anchor reported on the latest developments in the political arena.
The investigative journalist reported extensively on the corruption scandal.
Right now, the reporter is reporting live from the scene of the accident.

senses.definition: a TV or radio program made to entertain people

examples.x: Saturday Night Live is a sketch comedy show that has been on the air for decades.
The cooking show features chefs competing against each other to create the best dishes.

senses.definition: a TV or radio show, broadcast regularly, dealing with the routine life of a group of people and their problems

examples.x: She never misses an episode of her favorite soap opera.
The soap opera's dramatic twists keep viewers coming back for more.
He used to listen to soap operas on the radio with his grandmother.
The soap opera has been on air for over twenty years, captivating audiences with its ongoing storyline.
The characters' struggles in the soap opera feel so real and relatable to many viewers.

senses.definition: a room or building where TV or radio programs are made or broadcast

examples.x: The TV studio bustled with activity as crew members prepared for the live broadcast of the morning news.
The radio studio featured state-of-the-art soundboards and recording booths, where DJs entertained listeners with music and talk shows.
Actors rehearsed their lines on the soundstage of the film studio, surrounded by elaborate sets and props.
Podcasters recorded episodes in the makeshift studio set up in their spare bedroom, using portable microphones and editing software.

senses.definition: to watch something such as a movie or show

examples.x: We viewed the latest blockbuster movie at the theater last night.
She views her favorite TV show every Friday evening without fail.
They viewed the documentary about marine life at the museum's IMAX theater.
He plans to view the entire series of his favorite sitcom over the weekend.
We viewed the classic film on the big screen during the film festival.

senses.definition: an individual who watches content, such as videos, TV programs, or live streams, through traditional broadcasting channels or digital platforms

examples.x: As a loyal viewer of the show, Sarah eagerly anticipates each new episode and enjoys discussing it with friends afterward.
Viewer feedback plays an essential role in shaping the direction of the network's programming, helping to ensure it resonates with its audience.

senses.definition: a group of people who have gathered to watch and listen to a play, concert, etc.

examples.x: He was nervous to speak in front of a large audience.
She scanned the audience looking for her family.
The audience clapped loudly at the end of the performance.
The audience was moved by the powerful speech.
The theater was filled with an excited audience.

senses.definition: to pay some money in advance to use or receive something regularly

examples.x: Customers may subscribe to meal kit services for convenient and regular deliveries.

senses.definition: to make something known publicly, usually for commercial purposes

examples.x: The new movie release was heavily advertised through television, social media, and billboards.
Small businesses may use online platforms to efficiently advertise their offerings to a targeted audience.
Events and promotions are commonly advertised through posters, flyers, and digital channels.
The restaurant strategically advertised its special deals to attract more patrons.
The company is currently advertising its new product launch to a global audience.
